task_yaml.task:
I want to see whether you can seriously play an H5 mini-game. Open this 2048 page: https://hyhang.github.io/2048/ . The goal is that you must complete 2048. Reaching a high tile is the main thing I care about, and if you do not reach 2048 in the end, that part of the score scales with your final highest tile divided by 2048. But I also care about how you actually played: whether there is real gameplay (not just an opening screenshot), whether your final report is complete, and whether you played honestly without tampering with the board.

Do not just screenshot the starting board and stop; really try to play. You may decide the strategy yourself during play. At the end, save the highest tile, score, final-board screenshot, and key progress screenshots to `/tmp_workspace/results/2048_result.md` and images under `/tmp_workspace/results/`.

prompt:
I want to see whether you can seriously play an H5 mini-game. Open this 2048 page: https://hyhang.github.io/2048/ . The goal is that you must complete 2048. Reaching a high tile is the main thing I care about, and if you do not reach 2048 in the end, that part of the score scales with your final highest tile divided by 2048. But I also care about how you actually played: whether there is real gameplay (not just an opening screenshot), whether your final report is complete, and whether you played honestly without tampering with the board.

Do not just screenshot the starting board and stop; really try to play. You may decide the strategy yourself during play. At the end, save the highest tile, score, final-board screenshot, and key progress screenshots to `/tmp_workspace/results/2048_result.md` and images under `/tmp_workspace/results/`.
